design_philosophy.core_loop: Command squad of up to 6 soldiers through top-down missions using point-and-click positioning, tactical squad splitting, and real-time shooting against numerically superior enemies. Manage limited explosive ammunition via supply crates to destroy buildings and vehicles while preserving soldier lives.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited grenades/rockets) vs. mission objectives + fragile soldiers vs. overwhelming enemy numbers. Risk/reward of shooting crates to destroy targets vs. retrieving them for later use.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Military strategist directing small elite squad with superior firepower through dangerous missions, using tactics and positioning to overcome numerical disadvantage.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Darkly humorous anti-war tone that commentators variously praised and condemned. Game employs strategy game UI conventions (point-and-click, icons) uncommon to shoot 'em ups, deliberately blending genres. Developer intentionally rejected complex individual soldier attributes in favor of 'instant action' over 'war game' complexity. Tone and message created controversy (Daily Star criticism) despite critical acclaim.

difficulty.lives_system: Squad members are heavily fragile and easily killed; player must protect soldiers through careful positioning and tactical squad splitting. No explicit lives system documented, but soldier loss is permanent per mission.
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based progression through 24 missions divided into multiple levels (72 levels total). Implied checkpoint at mission start.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player must exercise caution and careful planning due to being outnumbered. Can split squad into smaller units to reduce risk or concentrate forces. Can choose to shoot crates (risky to soldiers) vs. retrieve them (safer). Enemy infantry becomes progressively more powerful across game progression.

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon arsenal progression: machine guns (standard, single-round kills vs. infantry) supplement with limited grenades and rockets required for buildings and vehicles. No explicit character/persistent upgrades documented.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear mission progression through 24 missions across various themed settings (jungle, snow, desert). Terrain hazards and new vehicle types introduced sequentially (rivers, quicksand, mines, igloos, snowmobiles).
replayability_hooks: Varied terrain and environmental hazards (rivers slow troops and prevent firing, quicksand, mines, booby traps)
Multiple mission settings and unique vehicles per environment
Strategic planning depth: different solutions via squad splitting and positioning
Ammunition scarcity creates variable difficulty based on player ammunition management

world_building_techniques: Military-themed mission structure with varied geographic settings (jungle, snow, desert)
Enemy variety (infantry, jeeps, tanks, helicopters, missile turrets, spawning buildings)
Environmental features that evoke specific regions (igloos, snowmobiles in snow levels; rural inspiration from Emmerdale Farm)
Darkly humorous tone embedded in gameplay and presentation communicating anti-war message

faction_indicators: Player-controlled squad presented as professional military unit with numbered soldiers
Enemy forces as distinct military antagonists (infantry, vehicles, installations)
Mission-based objectives suggesting military hierarchy/command structure
danger_signals: Fragile soldiers easily killed in single hit (both player and enemy)
Overwhelming numerical enemy advantage throughout game
Progressive difficulty via increasingly powerful enemy infantry
Environmental hazards (mines, booby traps, quicksand) that cause soldier casualties
environmental_storytelling: Varied terrain types and architecture convey different mission theaters (jungle temples, snow bases, desert installations)
Enemy building spawning mechanics suggest fortified positions
Vehicle deployments (tanks, helicopters) suggest organized enemy military structure
Rural and environmental details (Emmerdale-inspired settings) ground missions in specific locations
